---
title: "Cleaning 1"
author: "Raja Chinnakotla"
date: "2/28/26"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyr)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

>> Cleaning the data for languages

```{r}
language <- read_excel("language_mother_tounge_city.xls")
language<- language %>% select(-...9, -...10, -...12, -...13, -...15, -...16)
language <- language[-1, ]
colnames(language) <- language[1, ]
language <- language[-1, ]
language <- language %>% drop_na() %>%
  filter(grepl("000$", MOTHER)) %>%
  group_by(TOWN) %>%
  mutate(TOTAL = as.numeric(TOTAL),
    PERCENT = round(TOTAL/sum(TOTAL) * 100, 5))
View(language)

lang_dist <- unique(language$`AREA NAME`)
```

```{r}
towns <- read_excel("town_directory.xls")
towns <- towns %>% select(DIST_CODE, THSIL_CODE, TOWN_CODE, TOWN_NAME, CLASS, C_STATUS, AREA, POP_2001, DENSITY) %>% rename(TOWN = TOWN_CODE) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  mutate(AREA = as.numeric(AREA),
         POP_2001 = as.numeric(POP_2001),
         DENSITY = as.numeric(DENSITY))
View(towns)

```


```{r}


# Re-read skipping the messy multi-row headers
raw <- read_excel("religion_town.xls", col_names = FALSE, skip = 4)

# ── 2. Assign clean column names ─────────────────────────────────────────────
col_names <- c(
  "table_name", "state_code", "dist_code", "tehsil_code", "town_code", "area_name",
  "total_rural_urban",
  "total_persons", "total_males", "total_females",
  "hindu_persons", "hindu_males", "hindu_females",
  "muslim_persons", "muslim_males", "muslim_females",
  "christian_persons", "christian_males", "christian_females",
  "sikh_persons", "sikh_males", "sikh_females",
  "buddhist_persons", "buddhist_males", "buddhist_females",
  "jain_persons", "jain_males", "jain_females",
  "other_religion_persons", "other_religion_males", "other_religion_females",
  "religion_not_stated_persons", "religion_not_stated_males", "religion_not_stated_females"
)
colnames(raw) <- col_names

# ── 3. Clean: remove NA rows, number rows, Gujarat state-level rows ───────────
religion_clean <- raw %>%
  # Remove rows where area_name is NA or blank
  filter(!is.na(area_name), area_name != "") %>%
  # Remove leftover header rows (the column-number row)
  filter(!total_persons %in% c("1", "Persons")) %>%
  # Convert codes to numeric so filtering works correctly
  mutate(
    dist_code   = as.numeric(dist_code),
    state_code  = as.numeric(state_code),
    tehsil_code = as.numeric(tehsil_code),
    town_code   = as.numeric(town_code)
  ) %>%
  # Remove Gujarat state-level rows (dist_code == 0)
  filter(dist_code != 0 & town_code != 0) %>%
  # Convert all population columns to numeric
  mutate(across(total_persons:religion_not_stated_females, as.numeric))

# ── 4. Preview ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
View(religion_clean)
glimpse(religion_clean)
```


```{r}
religion_clean <- religion_clean %>%
  mutate(town_code = as.numeric(town_code))

language <- language %>%
  mutate(TOWN = as.numeric(TOWN))

town_dir <- towns %>%
  mutate(TOWN = as.numeric(TOWN))

# ── Join all three dataframes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
combined <- religion_clean %>%
  inner_join(language, by = c("town_code" = "TOWN")) %>%
  inner_join(town_dir,  by = c("town_code" = "TOWN"))

# ── Preview ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
glimpse(combined)
View(combined)
```


> working to combine basically everything ugh

```{r}
clean_combine <- combined %>% select(table_name, area_name, town_code, state_code, dist_code, total_persons, hindu_persons, muslim_persons, christian_persons, sikh_persons, jain_persons, buddhist_persons, jain_persons, other_religion_persons, `MOTHER TONGUE`, PERCENT, AREA) %>%
  group_by(town_code) %>%
  mutate(pop_per_person = total_persons / AREA) %>%
  drop_na()

View(clean_combine)
```

```{r}
riots <- read.csv("communal_riots_table.csv") %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~replace_na(., 0))) %>% mutate(dist_code = case_when(
    District %in% c("A'bad City", "A'bad Rural")         ~ 7,
    District %in% c("Vadodara City", "Vadodara Rural", 
                    "Westpol Vadodara")                   ~ 19,
    District %in% c("Surat City", "Surat Rural")         ~ 18,
    District %in% c("Rajkot City", "Rajkot Rural")       ~ 22,
    District == "Anand"                                   ~ 2,
    District == "Godhra"                                  ~ 16,
    District == "Sabarkantha"                             ~ 23,
    District == "Kheda"                                   ~ 10,
    District == "Mehsana"                                 ~ 13,
    District == "Banaskantha"                             ~ 4,
    District == "Bharuch"                                 ~ 5,
    District == "Gandhinagar"                             ~ 6,
    District == "Amreli"                                  ~ 1,
    District == "Junagadh"                                ~ 9,
    District == "Dahod"                                   ~ 8,
    District == "Narmada"                                 ~ 14,
    District == "Patan"                                   ~ 17,
    District == "Ahwa-Dang"                               ~ 26,
    District == "Jamnagar"                                ~ 11,
    District == "Navsari"                                 ~ 15,
    District == "Porbandar"                               ~ 20,
    District == "Valsad"                                  ~ 25,
    District == "Surendranagar"                           ~ 24,
    District == "Bhavnagar"                               ~ 3,
    District == "Kutch-Bhuj"                              ~ 12,
    TRUE ~ NA_real_
  ))  %>%
  filter(Sr_No != "Total") %>%          # remove total row
  mutate(District = case_when(
    District %in% c("A'bad City", "A'bad Rural")             ~ "Ahmedabad",
    District %in% c("Vadodara City", "Vadodara Rural",
                    "Westpol Vadodara")                       ~ "Vadodara",
    District %in% c("Surat City", "Surat Rural")             ~ "Surat",
    District %in% c("Rajkot City", "Rajkot Rural")           ~ "Rajkot",
    TRUE ~ District
  )) %>%
  group_by(dist_code, District) %>%
  summarise(
    Dargah       = sum(Dargah,       na.rm = TRUE),
    Masjid       = sum(Masjid,       na.rm = TRUE),
    Temple       = sum(Temple,       na.rm = TRUE),
    Church       = sum(Church,       na.rm = TRUE),
    Total        = sum(Total,        na.rm = TRUE),
    Madresa      = sum(Madresa,      na.rm = TRUE),
    Repaired     = sum(Repaired,     na.rm = TRUE),
    Not_Repaired = sum(Not_Repaired, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )


View(riots)
```

```{r}
final <- clean_combine %>%
  inner_join(riots, by = "dist_code")

View(final)
```


```{r}
final %>%
  group_by(District, dist_code) %>%
  summarise(
    total_persons = sum(total_persons, na.rm = TRUE),
    Masjid        = first(Masjid),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = log(total_persons), y = Masjid, label = District)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 3) +
  geom_text(vjust = -0.8, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "District Population vs Masjids Destroyed (2002)",
    x     = "Total Population",
    y     = "Masjids Destroyed"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm")
```
